title: International Accounting Lead
description: Who we are About Stripe Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. About the team Our finance team is responsible for shaping our understanding of how Stripe is performing as a business, as well as what our best opportunities are for continued growth. We’re looking for an experienced financial controller to join our International Accounting team. As a key part of our controllership team in Dublin, you will help us build our technical accounting and financial reporting processes, work with business partners, financial partners, external stakeholders and our engineers. You will help us develop best in class accounting processes and help track our progress within a fast-paced environment that’s growing globally. Role: International Accounting Lead What You’ll Do Responsibilities Financial Operations & Reporting Lead the Financial Close: Manage the end-to-end monthly close of US GAAP books, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and timeliness. Manage Reporting Deliverables: Oversee the preparation and submission of comprehensive regulatory and financial reporting. Own the Statutory Audit Process: Lead financial statement preparation, audit support, and technical documentation, culminating in presentations to the Board of Directors. Ensure Regulatory Compliance: Proactively monitor and adapt to changes in global accounting and tax regulations to maintain strict compliance. Oversee & support preparation of direct and indirect tax returns. Leadership & Strategic Collaboration Develop the Team: Mentor, guide, and manage the professional progression of a high-performing controllership team. Drive Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner effectively with Tax, Transfer Pricing, Treasury, Finance and Strategy, and Legal teams on diverse strategic projects. Act as a Business Partner: Develop a deep understanding of the Company’s transactions and contracts to properly advise cross-functional teams. Manage External Partners: Oversee and optimize our relationships with finance outsourcing vendors and auditors. Technical Accounting & Innovation Provide Technical Expertise: Maintain fluency in US GAAP and IFRS/FRS102, researching complex issues and producing clear technical accounting memos. Champion AI Adoption: Act as an innovation leader by evaluating, recommending, and implementing AI solutions to drive measurable process improvements across teams. Who You Are Minimum Requirements Professional Certification: Active ACA, ACCA, CIMA, or equivalent. Experience: 10+ years of progressive accounting or audit experience; Big 4, payments, financial services, or tech industry experience highly preferred. Technical Accounting: Demonstrated technical expertise and practical application of IFRS, FRS 102, and US GAAP. Audit & Tax Expertise: Proven experience managing large-scale statutory audits involving central and local teams, as well as overseeing tax filings. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. About the team Payments is Stripe’s flagship product. Our payments platform processes trillions of dollars annually. This team has the opportunity to expand the reach of Stripe’s global payments network, design and implement novel payment capabilities, and deliver best-in-class reliability and performance. We have Software Engineers in almost every team across Stripe, and in this role, you’ll be making some of the most significant decisions for the company. You’ll get to work with other engineers to build delightful features that span various parts of the system, as well as our business, sales and operations teams to understand and solve our users’ pain points. title: Software Engineer, Fee Insights
description: Who we are About Stripe Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. About the team Fee Insights builds fee explainability experiences that help merchants and internal teams understand what they're paying and why. We tackle this through agentic systems that provide natural language explanations grounded in canonical fee data, self-serve tooling, and also experiences that help users reconcile and understand complex pricing at scale. Fee transparency and explainability are critical to merchant trust and satisfaction. As Stripe's product portfolio and pricing complexity has grown, helping users understand their fees—and how they align with their contract and product usage—has become increasingly important. We're solving fundamental questions around fee transparency, building the foundations that will scale across Stripe's merchant base. What you’ll do As a Software Engineer on Fee Insights, you'll work on crafting billing-related experiences for both internal teams and external merchants. You'll partner closely with product teams to build reporting, dashboards, insights, and guided user journeys that help users interact with pricing and understand fees. You’ll be navigating one of Stripe's most complex domains, gaining deep end-to-end understanding of how Stripe works—from payments to pricing to billing. Responsibilities Building and iterating on fee explainability experiences that empower users to find answers independently Developing agentic systems with strong accuracy guarantees—building evaluation frameworks and ensuring reliability at scale Creating performant data aggregations and optimizations to handle complex pricing scenarios Working across the stack: backend services, frontend experiences, data pipelines, and AI agents Collaborating with stakeholders across engineering, product, operations, finance, accounting, and sales Independently identifying solutions to user pain points and executing against roadmaps Mentoring junior engineers and raising the technical bar for the team Who you are We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. Minimum requirements 5+ years of professional experience in a software development role Strong coding skills in any programming language Ability to drive and lead large-scale initiatives that solve critical business challenges Strong collaboration skills across teams and workstreams Track record of developing and delivering high-quality software using industry best practices Ability to make effective tradeoffs that balance business priorities, user experience, and technical sustainability

title: Finance & Strategy Lead, Payments Product
location: Seattle, Chicago, San Francisco, New York
description: Who we are About Stripe Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies - from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups - use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. About the team The Finance & Strategy team is a key thought partner to Stripe’s product leaders. We bring financial rigor, strategic clarity, and analytical depth to the decisions that shape Stripe’s product portfolio and long-term growth strategy. The Payments Products Finance & Strategy team sits at the intersection of Payments and Agentic Commerce. The team is increasingly evaluating opportunities across the entire Stripe Payments portfolio with rapidly evolving market dynamics as new agentic commerce tools and behaviors form. These areas require a finance leader who can operate across different stages of maturity, bring structure to complexity, and drive financial outcomes in an ambiguous environment. What you’ll do We're seeking a seasoned finance leader to lead our Agentic Commerce ambitions, both internally and externally. You’ll partner with product and engineering leads for Agentic Commerce, acting as a trusted financial and strategic advisor. You’ll be a thought partner in developing frameworks for how to assess the evolving landscape, identify areas for growth and drive investment frameworks / strategic insights that enable Stripe to best capture this opportunity. This is a highly cross-functional role and requires the ability to understand the different needs in each organization and translate technical language into concise commercial insights. Moreover, you’ll build the analytical infrastructure leveraging AI and tooling, to ensure this team delivers system-level improvements in how we deliver insights going forward. Responsibilities Act as a financial and strategic advisor to the business, building investment cases and business plans, and evaluating business performance. Create executive-ready presentations that tell the “investor narrative” taking into account internal data as well as external resources Support new business / product launches, helping drive strategy and decision-making by bringing rigorous driver-based financial analyses / frameworks and product and industry expertise to help product leaders evaluate tradeoffs and shape strategic roadmaps. As the business matures, establish and maintain key business metrics and KPIs to measure performance of new products. Lead the quarterly forecast and annual strategic financial planning process. Develop long range financial models with scenario analysis and actionable recommendations on growth, monetization and where to prioritize investments. Present performance in business forums, highlighting drivers, risks and actionable insights. Drive process improvement and analytics scalability, partnering cross-functionally to evolve data infrastructure, reporting and analytics. Identify opportunities to leverage AI and automation to enable the team to operate at the leading edge of financial analysis. Who you are We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. Minimum requirements At least 10+ years of relevant experience within strategic finance, ideally at a high-growth technology company and/or with prior experience in hedge funds, sell-side, or VC with strong analytical background (i.e. investment banking) B.A. or B.S degree in related field Exceptional analytical capabilities and business acumen, with the ability to simplify and distill complex information in a rigorous but consumable way Ability to create polished, executive- and investor-ready presentations that communicate complex narratives clearly and concisely with high impact Broad exposure to multiple industries and sectors; experience in technology, SaaS, or AI is a plus Proven ability to build strong collaborative working relationships with business partners and executives; you are seen as a trusted and objective advisor An enthusiastic self-starter with a "roll up your sleeves" mentality Ability to manage multiple work streams and maintain high performance under tight timelines Ability to deal effectively with ambiguity and thrive in an unstructured, fast-moving environment Experience with SQL or a willingness to learn Excellent verbal and written communication skills Preferred qualifications Deep understanding of payment and card networks economics Experience as a finance business partner supporting a partnerships or deal-making function

title: Backend Engineer, Expansion
description: Who we are About Stripe Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. About the team Web Presence and Platform is organized into two pillars, each of which is grouped into pods that focus on the central tenets of Stripe's public mission. The Presence pillar creates industry-leading designs for Stripe’s front door surfaces, educating customers about the power of our platform, sharing Stripe’s ideas and expertise with the public, and driving adoption. The Platform pillar builds the internal machinery that powers these surfaces, and is responsible for making our websites fast, stable, and easy to update. Together we design and build stripe.com and other sites that amount to what is, for many, their first impression of Stripe. As such, WPP offers exciting opportunities to have a major impact on Stripe’s success. We want to make every pixel count, we want it to be enthralling, and we want to help other Stripes seamlessly benefit from our systematic work. While the team's mission anchors to web surfaces, this role lives deeper in the stack. You will be the engine behind the experiences rather than the pixel-level presentation — APIs, data pipelines, model orchestration, and service reliability that make LLM-driven user experiences possible at scale. What you’ll do The Expansion pod brings creativity and executional rigor to attracting new prospects and driving conversion, focusing on user journeys, interactive and highly polished tools for users, and building targeted experiences for our global users. As a backend engineer you will architect and build the server-side systems and LLM orchestration layers that power intelligent, dynamic user experiences across acquisition journeys. You will design the APIs, data pipelines, and inference infrastructure behind delightful and original interactive tools and resources that attract users. You will collaborate deeply across design, engineering, and cross-functional teams and work across teams and tech stacks to create new site capabilities — with a specific focus on integrating large language models into production-grade services that are reliable, performant, and safe. This is not a research or ML training role. You will be responsible for wiring LLMs into real user-facing products: managing prompt pipelines, handling model routing and fallback logic, implementing guardrails and output validation. Responsibilities Develop and implement the tooling and platforms to support interactive content tools (e.g., calculators, generators, templates) that provide utility to current and prospective businesses. Utilize a user-first mindset to implement feedback mechanisms for continuous improvement of content and product offerings. Design and build integrations to third-party software and create seamless workflows across these tools to unleash their full potential to the rest of Stripe. Ensure reliability, security, and responsible AI practices across all LLM-driven features, including implementing content filtering, PII handling, rate limiting, graceful degradation, and output guardrails. Work with a small team of technically sophisticated engineers who put users first. Who you are We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. Minimum requirements 5+ years of backend development experience, with emphasis on building APIs and services that power user-facing experiences Experience writing clear, elegant code in a team environment Familiarity with LLM APIs and concepts — prompt engineering, token management, embedding models, retrieval-augmented generation, or function calling. You don't need to be a researcher, but you should understand how these models work well enough to build production systems around them. Experience building and operating distributed backend systems — you're comfortable with service-oriented architectures, async processing, caching layers, and monitoring/alerting in production. Passion for engineering solutions focused around growth hacking & enablement Excellent verbal and written communication Preferred qualifications Proven ability to stitch different services and processes together even if you have not worked with them before Experience in developing interactive tools and integrating them with existing systems. Experience building highly complex interactive tools, including state management and data fetching Experience with data pipeline orchestration (Airflow, Dagster, Temporal, or similar) and structured output parsing from LLMs Experience with prompt versioning, A/B testing model outputs, or building model gateway/routing layers that allow teams to swap or compare providers (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, open-source models) Proficiency with React, with a focus on interactions, animation, performance, and polish Experience with A/B testing, synthetic monitoring, or accessibility testing

title: Security Engineer - Offensive Security
description: Who we are About Stripe Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world's largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. About the team The Proactive Threat team is responsible for identifying vulnerabilities and security weaknesses across Stripe's systems, applications, networks, and cloud infrastructure — before adversaries do. We operate as a hybrid offensive function: conducting penetration testing, emulating real-world threat actors through red team operations, and partnering closely with our defensive security teams to validate detection capabilities and improve Stripe's overall security posture. We are builders first. Our team develops custom tooling, automation frameworks, and internal platforms that scale our offensive capabilities and enable repeatable, high-fidelity assessments. We believe the best offensive security engineers are equal parts hacker and engineer. The team is distributed across the United States, primarily operating in Eastern and Pacific time zones, and collaborates regularly with security, engineering, and product stakeholders across Stripe — including teams in Europe and Asia. What you'll do As an Offensive Security Engineer on the Proactive Threat team, you will simulate the tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) of real-world adversaries to uncover security risks across Stripe's products and infrastructure. You'll conduct hands-on penetration testing, lead red team engagements, and collaborate with blue team counterparts to validate and improve detection and response capabilities. Your work will directly influence how Stripe builds, ships, and secures financial infrastructure used by millions of businesses worldwide. Beyond assessments, you'll design and build offensive tooling and automation that amplifies the team's impact. You'll leverage threat intelligence to prioritize testing efforts, contribute to incident investigations when needed, and act as a subject-matter expert for security initiatives across the company. title: Solutions Architect, SMB
description: Who we are About Stripe Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. About the team Stripe’s SMB Solutions Architecture (SA) team works with technical and business leaders (CTO/ CIO/ CEO/ Founders etc.), payments leaders and product engineering teams in the SMB segment to evangelize Stripe’s solutions in the market. A solutions architect’s role is to build relationships with decision makers and think strategically about the business needs of Stripe’s customers. Solution Architects identify and propose Stripe solutions to solve complex business challenges and drive growth for our customers. SMB SAs establish and prescribe best practices around a customer’s strategy, provide business and technical thought leadership and are viewed as a trusted advisor. They work on scaling business processes aligned to maximizing account coverage while working closely with customer and partner resources to deliver a solution that can be adopted quickly. What you’ll do As a member of the team, the Solutions Architect will build highly consultative trusted relationships with c-level executives and technical decision makers in the customer’s organization to provide business and technical thought leadership, and become a trusted advisor in solving mission-critical business challenges. She/he will architect business models with them so they can drive new monetization opportunities and growth for both the user and Stripe. Responsibilities Drive thought leadership and build executive relationships with key business and technical stakeholders including CIO, CTO, COO, CRO, CDO & Head of Payments. Strive to become their trusted advisor Work to deeply understand the customer’s business objectives and apply Stripe’s payments, SaaS, Banking-as-a-Service and Link Network solutions to customer challenges Secure the technical win for Stripe’s products by delivering demos, responding to RFPs, evangelizing Stripe’s products, and collaborating internally on narratives that align with the customer’s goals Creating scalable processes (1:M customer engagements, Webinars, office hours etc), partnerships (with external and internal partners) and scaled assets (reusable videos/ templates) in order to act as a force-multiplier in the sales process Expand Stripe’s footprint within existing accounts to new global markets and business units by introducing new capabilities and local payment methods Collaborate with multiple internal stakeholders including go-to-market, product and marketing to deliver impactful EBC briefings for SMB accounts Act as the voice of the customer to inform our product team on insights, trends, and opportunities across our SMB accounts. Work closely with professional services and partners to accelerate activations and time to live Collaborate with partners to help Stripe scale and reach its Total Addressable Market Approximately 25% travel is anticipated to help you connect in-person in a meaningful way with users and collaborate internally Who you are An ideal candidate should have experience working for medium to large software companies (preferably in In payment-tech), focused on identifying business priorities, technology strategy, and solution design. They will have demonstrated the ability to become a trusted advisor to senior executives and facilitate customer success including business value identification(discovery/ demo cycles), road mapping, as well as advising and defining successful execution strategies. Ideal candidates have external customer-facing experience, have payment expertise (preferred but not required) who can present and architect Stripe’s solutions, and persuade decision makers to leverage Stripe technology to drive business value. Candidates have previous experience in web architecture, enterprise architecture or experience defining and optimizing financial business processes. Minimum requirements 5+ years of total relevant experience (software development, enterprise sales/ architecture, business development etc.) 2+ years of experience in a similar customer-facing engineering role (e.g. solutions architect, sales engineer, customer success, professional services delivery) for a fin-tech/payment-tech/global software enterprise Experience as a customer facing roles - presenting and architecting solutions. Domain experience in the payments space is preferred Deep understanding or background in SDLC and understanding of REST APIs. Experience in writing code in at least 2 of the following languages: Ruby, Python, JavaScript, Java, Go, etc. is preferred Outstanding oral and written communication skills, presentation skills, and comfortable explaining complex concepts to both technical and non-technical audiences Able and willing to learn Stripe solutions (Payments, Billing, Connect, etc.) Ability to understand how a wide variety of technologies and systems interact with each other (ERP, CRM, Billing, Tax, Legacy) Executive engagement skills and presence, with an ability to establish strong relationships with key decision makers and build credibility at all levels Strong organizational/time management skills and the ability to juggle competing priorities while working with multiple opportunities and customers Strong collaboration skills to work seamlessly with the broader GTM team to support our users Experience solving open ended business problems with a combination of technology and creative thinking Ability to travel per business needs

title: Head of Revenue and Growth, Canada
description: Who we are About Stripe Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies - from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups - use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. About the team The Canada Sales team is focused on driving revenue across the full spectrum of Stripe's customer base in Canada — from high-growth startups to some of the largest enterprises in the country. Stripe helps these businesses build new business models and drive innovation and modernization across industries. As the Head of Sales - Canada, you will be a critical part of the Americas go-to-market leadership team, owning the end-to-end sales strategy and execution for the Canadian market and driving accelerated adoption and revenue growth across Stripe's customer base in the region. What you’ll do Serve as the senior Stripe leader and executive sponsor for key customer and partner relationships across Canada, engaging C-suite stakeholders at Stripe's most strategic accounts. Own and drive sales growth across Canada through strong leadership, organizational planning, and outstanding execution of all sales programs. Oversee the expansion and satisfaction of Stripe's existing Canadian customers while aggressively pursuing new logo growth. Develop and refine Stripe's Canada-specific go-to-market strategy, guiding investment and resource decisions in partnership with Product, Engineering, Marketing, and other supporting teams. Make hiring decisions, shaping the ideal candidate profile and onboarding strategy to build a world-class Canadian sales organization. Create structure and focus for GTM ideas and product sales campaigns relevant to the Canadian market. Drive execution, measurement, and timely analysis with clear recommendations for action. Define key sales support mechanisms, processes, and programs to meet the rapid growth of the Canadian business and achieve revenue attainment and market development objectives. Build and develop a high-performing organization and help write the playbook for enterprise and commercial sales in Canada. Responsibilities Lead a senior, experienced team of Account Executives and indirectly lead the supporting roles and resources surrounding the accounts those AEs support across Canada. Develop the long-term vision and strategy for the Canadian market, scaling day-to-day operations to support growth. Champion and influence Stripe's sales methodology and process, driving best practices on selling, forecasting, and pipeline management across the team. Develop best practices that enable the Canadian organization to rapidly scale, deliver on revenue targets, and support KPIs for growth. Engage personally on large strategic opportunities, leading executive relationships and helping the team win and expand key customers. Effectively work cross-functionally across the organization to represent Canadian customer needs and help shape Stripe's product roadmap accordingly. Act as Stripe's market presence in Canada, engaging with the local business community, partners, and industry stakeholders to build brand awareness and strategic relationships. Who you are We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. Minimum requirements 5+ years of people management experience, ideally leading sales teams in Canada or broader North American markets Experience leading Sales organizations in a high-growth technology environment Experience building and scaling high-caliber teams, including launching or growing market presence in new regions Ability to hire, train, and coach a high-performance sales team Demonstrated quantitative and analytical skills with a strong command of pipeline and forecast management Deep understanding of the Canadian business landscape, including key industries, regulatory environment, and enterprise customer dynamics Preferred qualifications Prior experience at a growth-stage internet or software company Prior experience in enterprise sales, partnerships, and/or business development in Canada Prior experience in the payments industry, card issuing, or financial services Established executive-level network within the Canadian business community Bilingual (English/French) is an asset, particularly for engaging customers in Quebec

title: Engineering Manager, Managed Payments
description: Who we are About Stripe Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. About the team Managed Payments allows Stripe users to sell their products globally without the traditional overhead of tax, compliance, fraud, disputes, and more. By serving as the Merchant of Record for all transactions, we provide an all-in-one solution to seamlessly handle global digital commerce, allowing businesses to focus on growing their unique competitive advantages. We are a high-context, high-leverage team that operates at the intersection of almost every major product at Stripe. What you’ll do As an Engineering Manager for Stripe Managed Payments, you will lead a highly motivated team responsible for scaling one of Stripe’s most ambitious new product areas. You’ll work alongside leaders in the space to create a compelling vision, translate this vision into clear priorities, and work with your team to create surprisingly great experiences. This is a role for a strategic leader who excels at navigating complex challenges, both technical and non-technical. Your role will be uniquely cross-functional: success requires not only running a high-achieving engineering team, but also a willingness to dig into problems across domains. A typical week may involve supporting your team on ongoing engineering projects, exploring tax policy with domain experts, or creating staffing plans across multiple organizations to achieve outcomes. Responsibilities Lead and scale a high-performing engineering team, fostering a culture of high execution, rigorous prioritization, and open communication Define and drive the roadmap and priorities as the editor-in-chief of your team’s plans, collaborating with stakeholders across product, design, infrastructure, and marketing Establish close feedback loops with customers and collaborate closely with partner teams across multiple organizations Foster a high level of technical excellence on the team, upholding high engineering standards and implementing best practices throughout your team Recruit, coach, and mentor engineers, helping them grow their impact and navigate a fast-evolving product space Contribute to engineering-wide initiatives as a member of Stripe’s engineering management team Who you are We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. Minimum requirements 4+ years of engineering management experience, directly managing and growing teams of several engineers focused on building and shipping products at scale 8+ years of full time software development experience Experience building extensible, leveraged software solutions, with the ability to empathize with users and advocate for exceptional user experiences Technical acumen to engage thoughtfully with engineers around architecture and implementation decisions, serving as a force multiplier for the team Preferred requirements Track-record of thriving in cross-functional environments, demonstrating rigorous thinking, effective decision-making, and ability to build strong cross-team relationships Background in financial product companies and interest in financial products and markets Excellent communication skills, with the ability to influence partner teams and align them toward a shared vision Experience scaling early-stage products during critical periods of growth A strong product/design sense and a drive to find elegant solutions to complex problems

title: GTM Product Marketing Manager, Money as a Service (MaaS)
description: About Stripe Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies - from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups - use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. About the team The GTM Product Marketing team sits within Stripe's Product Marketing org. We make sure Stripe sellers walk into every conversation prepared, confident, and armed with the right story. We translate product value into seller-ready narratives, resources, and enablement — and we measure our impact on seller confidence and how effectively our teams pitch, qualify, and close. Stripe's Money as a Service (MaaS) products are redefining how platforms and businesses move, store, and manage money. We're looking for an exceptional GTM product marketer to bring Stripe's story to market. In this role, you'll be the connective tissue between product, marketing, and the field. You'll translate complex financial infrastructure capabilities into compelling narratives and seller resources that resonate with our users and drive adoption. You'll equip customer-facing teams to confidently pitch all our solutions – including Capital, Treasury, Issuing, Embedded Finance for Platforms, and Crypto/Stablecoins – answer technical questions, and speak to roadmap context. You'll also elevate seller and field insights to refine product positioning and messaging. What you’ll do As the GTM PMM for MaaS, you’ll need to quickly build a deep understanding of Stripe’s MaaS products, buyer personas, and differentiation. You will: Own the content development and GTM enablement strategy across the MaaS sold funnel — creating seller-ready resources (pitch decks, FAQs, value props, talking points) that translate product positioning into clear, differentiated customer value propositions for platforms, marketplaces, and enterprises. Partner with Market Intelligence to monitor and understand MaaS market trends, synthesize insights, and leverage these to build differentiated positioning. Design and deliver training programs that build seller expertise across the MaaS portfolio. Prepare sellers for major product launch events like Stripe Sessions, and to understand the details of the roadmap product is building. Leverage data and insights to identify enablement and knowledge gaps, prioritize asset development, launch new content, and measure effectiveness on seller confidence and other KPIs. Minimum requirements 8+ years of product marketing experience, preferably in B2B tech. Familiarity with fintech, payments, treasury, issuing, crypto and/or stablecoins, embedded finance, platforms and marketplaces, or financial infrastructure. Experience with Enterprise sales cycles and technical products. Excellent communication and presentation abilities, with a proven track record of distilling complex strategies into clear, compelling narratives. Strong stakeholder management skills. Ability to move quickly without compromising attention to detail or polish. A self-starter who thrives in fast-paced, ambiguous environments and can manage multiple priorities simultaneously. Comfort with data-driven decision making and measuring marketing impact. Proven ability to influence and align stakeholders across Product, Sales, and Marketing without direct authority. Preferred qualifications Demonstrated comfort working with, and presenting to, senior leaders across a variety of disciplines. Strong collaboration skills, a tendency to seek and share alternative views, and an ability to earn stakeholder confidence Proven past track record of building AI-powered tooling for sellers.
